review_text:
This is a good game to pick up. It's early access right now, so there are only two biomes and third one is supposed to be released in a few days. There is 20-30 hrs of content already in the game, as the nature of it is that you start as weak as you can be and through runs you gather stuff to unlock permanent perks that will put you in a good spot and level the playing field.

It's hack and slash. You have 2 slots for skills and an ultimate you charge through attacks. You start as a fast character, Flux, that has a teleport skill and soon after you unlock Ekku, the slow attacking heavy-hitter that is featured in the banner.

You will need to beat the first biome character to unlock the third crowd-control character who has pull, push area skills as well as locking 1 or more enemies. Vekta usually fights in the air.

Each character can choose between 2 skills for each slot they have, so in total they have 3 slots for 6 skills. Over time, you can choose your skills from the get go and upgrade them over the run. Each biome has two upgrade stations, one mid-way through the biome and one before facing the boss.

Bosses have 3 phases. First boss is OK but second boss has some really crazy setup right now. It's also buggy. Speaking of bugs, game has some that will kill your runs. Indeed not welcome but expected and accepted for early access game.

There are just a handful of people making this game, so for that kudos to them. Reddit is not active but if you need help or want to vent, discord has some regulars. Good luck and enjoy!

Absolutely Amazing. This game is as amazing as we all thought Anthem would be after seeing the first trailer ( no you dont fly around like ironman in this game) its NON-STOP action done in a thoughtful, artistic,  fun,and unique way. 10/10 easily if you love action rogue like game. loads and loads to do and upgrade.
it is SO FUN AND CLEAN, when they start to add content like more characters, upgrades in runs, and permanent buffs, it will be so amazing. new moves would be brilliant and set builds.

I think the game is very good for what it is. The combat loop is nice. But personally, I don't think there's much variety in the loop, and for me it's essentially just a rough like game where I can spend hours. 
In that game, there isn't much tactical variety; it's the same loop: kill, upgrade, repeat. But the upgrades don't change how you need to play the run tactically or anything. 
If you enjoyed games like Risk of Rain 2, you'll love this game. But if you need tactical variety in a roguelike, like in Bataltro, you won't enjoy it for long.
Truly a very Original Game and one of the only Singleplayer games that i can actually enjoy without company
wow where has this been, why did no one tell me. 9/10 at least for how cheap it is and good/fluid the game feel is

Morbid Metal is a good game with interesting ideas but really needs some update. I'll still recommend it because I have faith in the devs. The combat is very satisfying, but the thing is that you are stuck in the same gameplay loop very fast. You'll use the same build, fight the same bosses, in the same rooms. It needs to have way more diversed gameplay options to be a good roguelike, because right now you'll get bored after 10 hours max. And roguelike are good because of one thing : their replayability. Maybe some new characters to be able to change who you choose as your trio, some new powers for each characters, maybe some skins, some new perks that changes how a character is played for example. But the game is in early access so... let's wait I guess. Also, the game doesn't run bad by any means, but I feel like it could use a bit of optimization.

TL:DR : Some great ideas overall, but needs to be updated ASAP because of the lack of content and replayability.
best 20$ spent on a game in awhile wish it had multiplayer, but the idea around 3 different characters where you have to understand how to fight is mind blowing definitely worth  it

This game is in pre-alpha than in early access state. Below are some of the Pro and cons of the game

Pros
- DMC/Nier fans -> this game feel right at home 
- Good core gameplay loop 
- Good performance 
Cons
- too many bugs (e.g. if you press skill with multiple usage too quick, it will consume the usage but nothing happen)
- For a roguelite game the game is not randomize enough, it feel like there are only 2-3 pre-assigned route
- Normal attack is too plain -> could add things like combo or skill like in DMC to add a be more complexity in the game -> Otherwise it just feel like a chinese mobile gatch game (just consume skill then switch to another character

Anyways not recommend, the game is released too early to even be called early access

My new favorite roguelike, it is fun to an unreasonable degree its combos are awesome and feel great. The actual roguelike aspect is closely tide to what you pick rather than chance (chance is still a big part of the game though) and that's where strategy and planning come in to play, which i personally prefer as you can make some really insane combo extender builds that are super fun.

The game is similar to games like Devil may cry with its combo potential, Blazblue entropy effect in terms of roguelike builds, and character design like Warframe and Ghostrunner. 

and is not even a finished game yet (at the time of this review) its almost to good to be true. I cant wait to see what the devs do for this game.

It's a rogue like that gets boring pretty quickly. It's a fun game but you literally do the same gameplay loop over and over and over again. The only thing that differs are the pickups you get like upgrades to skills. The enemy placement is the same, the arenas are the same, etc. It gets boring.

An amazing game, even in this early access state. A great soundtrack, incredibly fun and addicting gameplay, super smooth animations, beautiful visuals and so much more. It took me 6 Hours to beat the first version of the game and even then I am 100% satisfied with my purchase, if this was a full release I would have been happy. I wholeheartedly recommend Morbid Metal. The only thing that I want to see from the devs now is more of this amazing game (;

personally, I like the concept and the combat is honestly addictive once you get used to the fast pace changing (hard to tell when i accidentally switch character mid combo but lead to sick moments lol). The game isnt perfect, but what can you expect from a early access game. I think the levels need to be more clear bc i do get confused if im progressing in the game or not.  that or add a checkpoint feature bc to get 22 mins deep into a level and die due to lack of heals available in game and to loose it all like that kinda blows. Other than that, i think this game can be really cool if taken the right way.
Bought this game just to listen Blue Stahli's background score. I truly enjoyed the music and the game. Futuristic  and fast paced game, very engaging! I would definitely recommend it.

Nice game, i need/want more !! 

Looks great, feels great - Great game.

the game is amazing so far but i have a issue im not sure if anyone else experiences i cannot close the game when hitting quit to desktop, it will fully freeze my screen on a black screen and say im still playing the game and im forced to restart my entire computer.

I love the pacing and mechanics! It feels a little bit bare bones at the moment, but has extreme potential. Even in its current form I'm having a ton a fun and will continue to sink hours in. Cant wait for future updates and characters!

The speed of Returnal and the combat mechanics of DMC (without a fixed camera,) this game is excellent for the price point. Once unlocking all 3 characters, basic attacks are rendered secondary to the 2 skills you'll get per character. You can rotate each to get some absolutely diabolical combos and the cinematic quality is terrific. I haven't found a "META" build yet but having extra charges always seem to be useful. It feels less grindy then other games as I was able to fill out the initial upgrade tree with relative ease. It's the red currency that is very hard to come by and I suppose that is the point. I'll gladly give my time to this as it checks all my personal boxes but would understand the criticism others have for its lack of variety. I'm just easy to please.

Hopefully this gets the polish and updates it deserves because it really nailed the "old school" video game combat feel. Just wish there was more to everything: paths (areas), upgrades, combos, enemies, BOSSES, playable characters, story, ect...

For now I wait.

8.5/10

was scrolling about , looking for something interesting or just to see maybe some funky deal~ , as one does when they're a lover of games and user of steam.
zip zip zap, dip, bippity bop. 
run across an interesting looking picture, says Morbid Metal (eXcuse mua, wata da faku is morbid metal) , Clicks.
scrolls pictures around, hmm, Action roguelike / hack and slash ... (<- loves roguelikes. ok, am more then curious)

next step for me, look up game name & gameplay , right.

instead I clicked on , official trailer, saw actual gameplay presented, at about 5 seconds in, already went back to steam, to grab it.

so what if The Lotus from Warframe and Dante from DmC had a very fun night, while Doom's theme was playing, and it was choreographed by Returnal.

usually, the games tend to shoot themself in the foot, for me, because, adhd lizard. hates tutorials.
I mean **HATES** tutorials, with the passion of 10thousand suns.
I have, and will probably again, remove games out of existance, that do overspam of pop up msgs, completely restricting any playability/movement because you want to shove a stupid block of text in my face. 

very important, for me, and i think for any gamer -> Curiousity.
Let.Me.Fuack.around.and find out.

Pretty fun for the most part, its still in early access when I'm writing this. I completed the game with 14 hours played. There's still some jank with the movement when platforming and the target lock feature is very annoying because it switches targets based on proximity and there's no way to configure the lock on feature. I ended up mostly having to navigate fights without locking on to my target until my character had gotten close enough to the enemy I want to target (I take out flyers ASAP in fights) and then I can lock on and kill them. 

Fast paced, fun progression, I can see this game being really good when it has some more time in the oven to bake. I still reccomend it for hack and slash/souls enjoyers if you can deal with the rouglike progression loop that sends you to the beginning after each run. It ran great on my PC

would genuinely be a 10/10 if the camera lock didn't suck ass. that thing may as well have been designed by a toddler on some sort of substance. then again, maybe i havent played for long enough to fully experience it.

still highly recommended, peak combat and a genuinely difficult gameplay loop will keep me coming back to this.

Solid roguelike gameplay loop, as long as they add way more ways to diversify the build you craft each run. Unfortunately, synergistic builds aren't very prevalent in the game, so "building" your loadout during a run is boring, despite the combat being absolutely incredible! Genuinely difficult at first, this game has stellar slow-motion action and feels tremendously fluid when it comes to movement, auto-targeting, and chaining/combo-ing abilities. Worth trying out for now, but I'll keep my expectations low.

Would say enjoyable gameplay only until you get to a boss, not sure if I'm doing something wrong but the amount of hp you have vs the amount of damage the bosses deal (+ first boss has 3 phases... why?) is wrong. 

Okay first boss very much possible, just gotta dodge and not be super aggressive.

Second even easier, just stack critical chance + critical damage + orbs + leak and it almost insta dies. Plus looks like there's only two bosses? All for now ig XD.

I think i had some weird movement interactions with the map and the music could be better.

I would say the game is a solid 8/10, maybe hack and slash is "too much about the same thing again" (you know, run, sword sword, kill, repeat).

I've only unlocked 1 character so far and did not manage to kill the first boss yet (5 attempts, guess i'm too noob), but after 3 hours playing this pair, its a mix about learning skill curve, combo synergy and getting a little of that "too much of the same" feeling, but again that could be cause of the poor progression due to my own noobness!

If you like Action/Hack and Slash, it's a 10/10 for you...

If you came looking for something to take, for example, Dead Cells to a 3D modern graphics upgrade version, like myself, maybe you are going (most certainly) to miss the depth of content Dead Cells brings (you know, thousands of possibilities with combinations of diff weapons/skills/traits). The map doesn't reward you much, you will find upgrade for your character's abilities or swap them along the rooms, 1 or 2 health potions here and there, coins for talent upgrades and that's it for the first map i guess.

Compared to Dead Cells, well, you get nice looking modern graphics and very good action but i would say it's a 6/10 here if you want the amount of variety Dead Cells offer in general.

I did enjoy the game, wish this to become an unforgettable video game, maybe future updates will bring us lot's of new possibilities of customizations!

Action/Hack and Slash - 10/10
"Dead Cells next gen" - 6/10

⚔️ Very fun and stylish roguelite with surprisingly smooth combat.

Flux feels amazing to play if you enjoy fast melee assassin gameplay with mobility, crits, blink attacks, and aggressive combat. The progression systems with protocols, routines, and permanent upgrades are actually deeper than I expected.

The cyber/sci-fi art style also looks fantastic, especially on ultrawide OLED at high refresh rates.

Since it’s still Early Access, I’d love to see:

• More characters
• More enemy variety
• Additional maps and bosses
• Expanded endgame progression

But the core gameplay loop already feels solid and responsive, which is the most important part for this type of game.

Definitely promising and worth keeping an eye on. 🔥
The newly added difficulty settings are great, and i recommend this game . great music, and the overall flow and feel of the controls is a huge plus too

It's like someone said what if DMC but robots, better camera, and Returnal's movement and progression right out of the gate.... for the price of a Crunchwrap Supreme and a Baja Blast. Welcome back, roguelite fans.

[b]TL;DR: 

If you like DMC/God of War, play this. 
If you like those and Returnal, play this. 
If you like Hades, play this. 

Great combat, good performance, good graphics, fun gameplay and it gets deeper with more playtime.[/b]

--------------------------------------------------

I don't usually post reviews but many of the negative reviews seem to only have <2 hours of playtime so they were able to refund it.. and honestly, the devs deserve a bit of praise for what they've made.

Without getting too deep into it/spoiling too much:

- The game does get deeper the more you play and you start figuring out builds you want for each of your characters. 

- [spoiler]Unlocking the 3rd character adds A LOT of variety to fighting.[/spoiler]

- The fighting is fluid and feels great, hits feel like they hit hard and once you get into the flow of combat you can really start doing massive damage/linking combos and attacks and clearing rooms without getting hit. 

- The Skill tree adds more to the game than just +Health or -Cooldown. I'm an idiot and completely forgot about it for a good 6 hours and was hitting my head against the wall trying to beat the first boss with no upgrades (it's definitely doable, so if you want a challenge then try that. After the first boss, I'm not sure how far you'd get without any upgrades).

- While the game is room-based progression, there are some pretty hidden pickups around so it's worth checking every little crack.

- The 'Trial' rooms only have about 3 types of challenges at the beginning, but as you get stronger more challenges will appear that can & will be harder than the first ones and also depending on what build/buffs/debuffs you're running.

[b]EDIT: After playing a bit more, the game is now changing up the first Biome by adding in more hazards (lasers)/even stronger enemies.[/b] 

- Failure never feels [i]bad[/i]. Every single time I've died, it's because I messed up by doing something at the wrong time. The game never feels unfair or like it's thrown a bunch of bull**** at you to make you lose.

- There are ways to mitigate RNG, start your run with abilities you want or have specific unlocks for you to pull from chests. 

- Performance is pretty good. Playing in 4K Ultra Settings and I get a pretty solid 90 fps.

The more I'm playing this game, the better it's becoming.

MORBID METAL has really solid combat. It uses a Devil May Cry style rank system and pulls it off well. You can switch between 3 characters and create a lot of combos, so it stays fun and doesn’t feel repetitive. Overall, it’s a fun game , definitely worth trying.

Hmm, perhaps released too early, even for early access. The game is fun, but needs lots of rebalancing. 

Right now the main problem is the repetitive combat. It's advertised as a DMC like, but in reality plays more like RoR2. They need to add a heavy attack and add standard hack n slash combos. Currently you have a light attack, but it is not fun nor useful. Gameplay loop is chaining skills between characters, then dodging till your get your cooldowns back. It's not bad per say, but it can be so much more.

The enemies need a lot of work though. Melee units are just there and can be literally ignored. You deal with the ranged ones, and then deal with the melee that can barely reach you. I would increase mob difficulty (NOT by making them hit harder / have more health) and reduce the mob count in each encounter.

By adding real combos, changing mob balance and adding more variety they can have something extremely fun. 

Bosses hit too hard while being too squishy. The encounters don't feel the best due to this. More work needs to be done here. 

Actual stages are fun and detailed. Soundtrack is good. Visuals are great. Performance is good.

Game is just lacking story & overall content for the moment being. Finished the full run within 7 attempts and less than 3 hours invested.

Looking forward to future updates.

Morbid Metal made a strong first impression on me visually. The graphics are beautiful, and during my first twenty minutes I found myself doing little more than looking around and admiring how the game looks. On a purely aesthetic level, it succeeds immediately.

What is harder to define is what the game actually feels like beyond that first impression. It is a hack-and-slash roguelike, almost like a strange mix between Devil May Cry 5 and Hades. At the same time, it does not really capture what made either of those games stand out. Both Hades and DMC5 had strong identities through their storytelling. Hades gave you a clear goal, memorable characters, and a constant sense that you wanted to learn more. DMC5 had a very deliberate story to tell. Morbid Metal, by contrast, feels vague and mysterious to the point where, at least early on, the narrative seems distant and unimportant. It throws you into the action with very little grounding, almost as if the game is saying: I do not know who I am, I do not know why I am here, all I know is that I must kill. Maybe the story develops later, but in the opening hours it lacks the hook that keeps the player emotionally invested.

Another issue is the roguelike structure, especially the RNG. In Hades, your choices mattered because you could choose which door to enter, shaping your run in a meaningful way. Here, the system feels much flatter. You get doors marked with an arrow, and that is about it. Instead of adding strategy, it feels like the game is giving you the illusion of choice without much actual control.

The enemies also do not leave much of an impression. So far they feel generic and uninspired, more like disposable targets than interesting opponents. They exist to be killed and not much more. There is little personality or creativity in their design, at least from what I have seen early in the game.

That said, despite all of these flaws, I still think Morbid Metal is a good game. It has clear shortcomings, but it also has potential. Since it is in early access, a lot of its rough edges are understandable, and the current state of the game definitely reflects that. The positive side of that is that it has room to grow, and every patch or update could genuinely make it better. I am glad I got to play it, and I plan to keep going until I either reach the end or lose interest.

For anyone wondering: yes, it works right out of the box on Linux. No issues so far, though I haven’t sunk a ton of hours into it yet.

P.S: Morbid Metal does not natively feature FSR 4. Instead, the game currently uses FSR 1 for upscaling.

Very Morbid, Much Metal. 10/10 would Juice all over my Screen again.

Yes, i waited for the game to release to write this.

Runs on laptop, which is insane optimization for 2026. Also a pretty cool game. Just mindless cathartic slashing. Personal banger.

People keep saying "Devil May Cry but with robots" and honestly that is not far off. You switch between three characters in real time, chain combos together, and the hits actually feel like they land. There is a ranking system for how stylishly you fight and that alone kept me trying to do better instead of just brute forcing through everything. The game runs beautifully, looks great, and switching up characters mid-fight opens up more combinations than I expected when I first started. Where it falls short right now is the enemies. Most of them are fairly generic and the melee ones especially feel like they are just there to fill space. Story exists but barely, there is not much pulling you forward narratively. The roguelite structure works but feels a bit shallow compared to what the combat system promises. It is Early Access though, and the foundation is genuinely strong. If the developers keep building on what is already here this could become something really good.

I have to double what other reviews have to say. The game looks good, feels good and plays well without any bugs I encountered so far. But there's just no variety to make it a fun game. There are about 2-3 builds you can make because all other upgrades you encounter just give you more of a general passive bonus than a viable addition to your build. And since 1-2 of the possible builds feel way less powerful than the other one there really is no reason not to always go for the same one.

The game could be a real fun play to frequently come back to but there just isn't any content to not get bored after 3-4 hours max because you feel like you've seen it all even without having completed the game.

- Exploring just doesn't really seem worth it right now so there feels like a lot of empty space between arenas.  Either need to make the rewards better, or tighten up distance between fights
- Needs more hit impact/notification when you take damage.  Game doesnt really give you any feedback that youve been hit (other than looking at the HP bar).  Too many times I felt confused as to when I took any damage after happening to look at my HP bar.
- More characters are actually worse for the player.  Don't get me wrong, it's super fun to cycle through characters to combo, but by adding characters to the rotation, you are actually bloating your upgrade/reward pool so they need to figure out a way to balance having multiple characters without making the rewards feel bad.

Maybe one of the best hack-and-slashes I've ever touched, fighting up there with games like Devil May Cry 3 for favourite game of the genre. I remember grabbing the demo back during one of the Steam Next Fests, and I was instantly blown away. So blown away, that I refused to delete the demo off my computer until the full game dropped. For a measly $24 CAD, or your regional equivalent, you'll be getting WAYYY more than you paid for

Even though there are only a total of three characters in the game right now, the level of depth and complexity for each one blows me away. While the basic combo strings equate to just hitting one button, it's what you can do in-between that really makes the game shine. You can switch from character to character, trading light and heavy hits while splicing in big attacks that shred health or knock the enemies airborne to immobilize them. And thats not including the character-specific upgrades to each character, letting them all fit into their little niches during your runs. Being stylish and getting up to that coveted SSS combo rank has almost never felt this great. Jumping from enemy to enemy, hacking and slashing and ending it all off with one powerful ultimate skill to clean house. 

Presentation is tickling my brain in such a delicious way. I'm a sucker for robots, and simulations, and stuff like that. The player characters are the epitome of cool, and it is totally making me wanna live out my fantasies of being a badass robo-slayer. Both the different biomes are so so so beautifully crafted, I'd be hard pressed to say that a set of indie devs did it. Whether you are in a huge chasm with small spires of overgrowth and rock while jumping around with grappling hooks, or in the derelict, broken down arenas where enemies reside, or if you're tucked away inside the darkness of the abyss, it's all so beautiful. It can get a bit repetitive on repeat playthroughs, but I personally don't have much problem. Music only elevates the feel of Morbid Metal further. These songs have been engraved into my brain and they make me feel the energy. The riff after clearing a wave is electric, I love it so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ much

There are little bits of lore fed to you to keep you engaged with the mystery of whats going on, and some other text files to clue you in on what is happening. Otherwise, story seems to be on the backburner for right now while the devs fine-tune the combat even more. This is not a bad thing, just a heads-up for anyone who might want a killer story already made to accompany this great game!

My only real issue has been some small bugs, that I'm sure will be ironed out in the following updates. Things like my characters being unable to dash forward, or my attack inputs being disabled until I switched character. They were few and far between, but it is something to keep in mind. If you hit all your buttons, it will likely fix itself. 

I know it was published by Ubisoft, but Morbid Metal is clearly a passion project from a group of hard-working indie devs. They deserve all the love and attention for sticking with their baby after all these years and giving the audience something great to sink our teeth into

Morbid Metal is a really competent arena brawler that doesn't really nail the "roguelite" part in any meaningful way. I see some people try to compare the combat to DMC but this is based on cooldowns and weapon switching. There are no multi-button combos or directional presses or parries. What is here is an absolute blast to play... until it sorta isn't.

Right now the nature of upgrades don't really feel as good as they could. It feels like there are pieces of a great build scattered around multiple systems which... either aren't fun to engage with or are just done better by other games (Returnal, Remnant, VS, etc).

To be fair to these devs, it's much harder trying to make a brawler like this feel fresh compared to a shooter. When you can't just throw in a bunch of guns and spells and effects to pad the content, you have a harder job ahead of you. I don't think the "exploration" of a very linear game like this is adding much just because an entire upgrade system is hidden in RNG chests. I would rather have more options to flesh out a build before a run than depend on yet another game of Pick 3 RNG from a list of effects.

It looks great, the animations and character design are gorgeous. However the camera is a mess to try to use, whether you target lock or not. It's too easy to lose sight of the entire battle because you did a warp attack or a dash. Some better camera intelligence would be really helpful here, especially considering how often you'll be moving across the arena.

Also, chromatic aberration sucks. It looks terrible in every game. Give us a toggle in the Options to turn it off, or stop using it. It really adds nothing to the visual quality of the game. There are also weird line effects when running that look like screen tearing. This should also be a toggle. I don't care if we're in a "simulation" these types of matrix effects are distracting.

We'll see what the roadmap has to offer and how this game shapes up over the next year and then I may give it another shot and change this review. But as of right now I don't think I can recommend this Early Access unless you really need the hack n slash catharsis because you've played literally every other brawler available and are itching for more.

Gameplay feels nice and fun, but everything else around it feels like it was made to annoy you. Boring roguelike options. Getting to store with 4 credits too low to buy anything. Boss fight phases where they just donkey kong barrels at you instead of actually fighting (the fun part??). Forced exploration to find another green version of the same 2 weapon skills. Laser jumping puzzles and fall reset damage just to slow down runs, forcing me to be careful rather than a ninja. Random invisible wall or hitboxes on places that /look/ like they might be part of the puzzle or hidden loot that just forces you to fall and lose hp, punishing exploration. Time slow down on a ton of weapon skills that move your camera (you're not invulnerable during this). Unreliable perfect dodge counters making the success noise, but offering you no counter attack. And you can't swap characters in the air (or something is sometimes preventing me, idk, couldnt figure it out, frustrating) in a game where you're in the air a ton, with different weapon skills having alternate attacks in the air. 

Combat trails that i experienced had really really annoying requirements. Not hard, just annoying. Like one was swap your char every 5s, simple enough. Except with the slowdowns and animation locks, ended up failing on the last enemy because I had to audacity to ult him without knowing how long the ult animation is, losing the run because I was unable to swap chars in the middle of it.

Game will probably be good in the future, but please get someone thats played a rougelike on the dev team. Hades, blazblue entropy effect, etc all have super fun and game changing stuff that make every round feel different. This game doesn't have that flavor.

And remove all the exploration/jumping puzzles. Theyre not fun and slow down the run for no reward.

This was a day one buy for me after playing the demo.

The levels themselves are fun to traverse but the game would benefit from a minimap, the environments tend to look a bit samey so it can get a bit disorienting. 
Overall, the core mechanics are solid but it needs more time to cook.

It's morbid time.

There's a good idea here, it just needs a lot of polish and smoothing out the rough edges.

Pros:
Combat's pretty smooth, Warframe-esque movement feels nice unless you're trying to loot something, the general gameplay loop is promising.

Cons:
You fight the same 4 enemies all the time. Some moves are awfully telegraphed - mostly in the boss fight, which is where the animations should be the best imo. The way the camera unlocks before the boss jumps on you is confusing, its animations are not telegraphing much, and it only has like 4 moves total.

Everything seems either very unbalanced, or as if they're trying to make you grind to pad the game time. You die in like 5 hits and while you can upgrade health, it takes a run or two and means you're sacrificing unlocking the more interesting options.

There isn't much content and the rooms already start repeating on the second run.

All of the issues can be fixed and the game's promising, but I can't recommend it as it is.

Metal May Cry

I was a big fan of the demo and couldn’t wait for release and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, it delivers.

This might be one of the most fun roguelites I’ve played in a while. Morbid Metal just feels good. The combat is smooth, the music is a banger (just like in the demo), and now it’s all spread across three distinct, really well-made regions. For the price, the visuals are honestly kind of crazy it genuinely looks like a AAA game at times. The overall style even gave me strong Metal Gear Rising vibes, which I absolutely love.

What stands out most is how good the combat feels immediately. Even sticking to one character is already fun. But once you unlock more and start shapeshifting mid-combat, things really open up. You can chain combos in a way that feels creative, not scripted — like you’re inventing your own flow instead of just executing preset strings. That’s where the game really shines.

Overall, this doesn’t even feel like an early access game — the level of polish is really high.

This game has the foundation of a good CAG roguelike but falls flat in many ways.

- The tag mechanic feels good but is lacking due to the paltry movesets. All you have is a basic attack that doesn't even feel good to use, two actives and a super. None of the characters feel like functional CAG characters on their own, with tagging required to do basic things like a launcher or helm breaker. Because of this the tagging can feel like a gimmick because you're basically switching through three limited CAG movesets that barely function and are on par with what even other indie CAG characters can do without any upgrades.

- Arbitrary cooldown system. With the moveset being so small, the gameplay loop is basically dumping cooldowns and using basic attacks. This gameplay loop reminds me of PGR and Aether Gazer at launch. which both games fixed over time. If they can get this combat to Pirate Warriors 4 level where the cooldowns still exist but there's also a functional normal moveset, it would be a lot better.

- Punshing and padded out traversal. Healing is already minimal and losing health because of failing platforming feels really bad. The level chunks are beautifully crafted but also far too big for the type of game this is. There's a weird amount of backtracking and empty space that serves no purpose other than to pad the game out. Cutting this stuff down would greatly increase the pacing.

- Odd difficulty spikes. The health bar is basically a myth and runs die before you even know what hit you. Right now it's relatively fine since maybe half of the content is in the game currently, but the unfair damage enemies do will definitely become a problem as the game gets longer. Feedback on getting hit is pretty rough and the insane damage enemies do doesn't help either.

- Uninteresting and limiting metaprogression. Most of the metaprogression is just stats while also having cool things like changing your starting moveset, but at no point do I ever feel like just having them all at once wouldn't help the otherwise limited combat feel better.

Overall this game has a lot of potential, but if you're looking for tag-based combat also made by small teams, here's some better options around the same price point.

Assault Spy
Genokids
Overrider

Check these out and hope this game get anywhere close to these games levels, haha.

Really cool! Very well presented, very aesthetically "cool" and the combat flows well and is fun. Think of it as Devil May Cry with cooldowns on your abilities.

The game is a bit of a slog to begin with when you only have 2 characters, but as soon as you unlock the third it gets a lot more enjoyable. Aerial enemies are super annoying and need to be killed first, as soon as you do that the game becomes a lot easier.

Gripes are -
Running back from getting a chest from an identical platforming parkour area over and over, might as well just put a teleporter at the end and teleport you back to the door.

Buttons randomly seem to stop working, ranging from jump, attack, and skills. You can see the skill button being pressed in the corner, so it's definitely a game issue.

Some enemy tells for when they're attacking aren't clear, like the guys with shields etc. Probably a me problem? But either way I do find that I quite often just explode and don't know what killed me.

Momentum gets kinda messed up, there was a jump that drained all my life because I just could not make it, as soon as I connected to the floating green platforming assistant it would just kill all my momentum.

First boss has a really boring phase where he constantly shoots at you and you dodge 5 times, then you do it again whenever he wants to do it. Also, lock-on keeps toggling off whenever he jumps after this phase. Goes on way too long in general.

In the second biome, the purple hurting liquid is pretty hard to spot as everything else is pretty pinky/purple already, and it's not pronounced enough to make it clear that "hey this will hurt you" until you've already been hurt by it. One of my biggest gripes in any roguelike is navigational hazards that are hard to spot, just kills enjoyment for me.

Maybe a second attack button? When all your skills are on cooldown it isn't very satisfying to just only have your normal attack to use.

Performance is okay, I stay above 100FPS at 1440p, but my 5090 gets used about 60% with DLSS on performance. Great looking game, just would like a few more FPS (framegen?).


OTHERWISE! Really fun and really cool, cheap, and my gripes can be easily addressed due to early access.
The game is fun, the mechanics are very unique and enjoyable, but the optimization is poor—it often stutters, and the world feels empty.

All around a pretty good game so far. I like the amount of content for this and am anticipating more; potentially couple characters more, a coop mode for either local or online and an actual story. In my honest opinion this game does a lot of things well. For example, the combat is INCREDIBLY fun, and the music is spot-on. However, there are a lot of glaring issues that make it hard to recommend, the main one being the roguelike features. At first they were cool, but after 20+ runs it gets boring seeing the same 3 or 4 builds that barely change your playstyle. There really should be more ways to change your playstyle in a meaningful way, otherwise there's no point in including them. Additionally, the hidden caches take away from the fast-paced combat and became tedious to track down when I was just itching to fight the next room. I also ran into a bug where the game soft locked after I used my ultimate to clear the 2nd boss.

All in all, I think this game really does have potential but it has a major identity problem with the core game design. Additionally, I feel it could be improved by removing all the side paths and hidden caches that make you backtrack through empty rooms that only give a sidegrade at best.

I would give this game a 6/10 in its current state but I may change my mind once improvements have been made.

Feels like rare Ubisoft win -- fun game for reasonable price without DRM.

Game definitely needs a little bit polishing: a stutter here and there, camera can behave erratically but this is what early access is for. 

Has a great potential for 1.0 release if developers will continue working on the game.

UPD. Played the game 20+ hourse more and it is an absolute combat crack -- very fun, very engaging and very satisfying. After some polish and more content game will be an absolute banger.

Morbid Metal...

If I could I would place the game more of an inbetween of recommend and don't recommend. But I'll lean towards recommend mainly because of it's generous price point.

Let's start with the good!
The combat feels good, and has the potential for a strong flow of powering up along your journey through the run.
The game concept is nice, having to swap constantly between different characters (Metals?) and doing your best to synergize their abilities to dish out as much damage as possible before having to dodge or switch to defensives.

The bad, and what needs improvements.
The roguelite elements... they need some SERIOUS work. To start, the permanent upgrades after every run are very weak, they don't impact the game or the runs much, and don't open the game up to a more diversity with how you want to build yourself and how what you want to grab.

Secondly, the in run upgrades are VERY lacking. Many of the chests will drop a new Skill (Protocols) that is rarely ever an upgraded version of what you currently have, and have a chance of dropping a skill that isn't your playstyle, You can't even recycle them to get currency, WHICH by the way, currency is as of now the best way to power-up. Shops and currency are THE go to way to become strong. And that's because all the worthwhile nodes that actually upgrade your stuff consistently have a currency cost.
All the passives (Routines and Corporas) have the chance of making your skills feel more indepth with how you start to dish out damage, but don't actually invite you to change how you want to use it. It's sort of benefits that don't change how you want to attack. but instead encourage you to stick to a certain set of skills because it won't work with them all.

Summary: Strong combat, weak rogue-lite elements. Loot needs to feel better and allow for more diversity.
Strongly recommend taking ideas from another phenomenal Rogue-lite that's still in Early Access called: Sephiria.
But the price point still makes the game worth trying and seeing where it goes!

Played the demo and loved it, but Early Access completely sold me — this game is 100% worth it.

The combat is fast, smooth, and super satisfying, and the character-switching mechanic adds a really fun layer of strategy. It feels great once you get into the flow of the intended fighting style / mechanic of the game.
This game is 10/10 Freaking amazing! Its less then a Big Arch Value meal from Mcdonalds... So its not Arching your wallet like a lot of these other games.

In what I played so far I am enjoying it. I have no issues at the moment. When I do experience any bugs or what have you I will updated this review. I would recommend this game for those like fact pace hack and slash.
Epic, i would like it even more if music was more aggressive in there but gameplay is smooth even on a keyboard and mouse

It takes a lot of trust to endorse something that's not only technically unfinished, but also from a developer who is new without much of an established reputation yet. With that being said, there's a lot they have done right, continue to do right and at this point, I have enough trust in their ability to fulfill their promises to where I can leave a positive review, knowing they'll continue to do right.

I'll list off the few things I don't like, as the majority of this review will just be gush.

Cons:
-Lack of checkpoints. I know it's a roguelite, in that there's incentive to replayability, but that doesn't mean it shouldn't be inaccessible to those with busy schedules. There needs to be some way to quickly get back to where I was if checkpoints are out of question. The game is short, but not short enough to where I'm always willing to sit down and invest in it. I don't always care about pulling off sick moves, I don't want every game I play to become a chore, I want to just trust that I can turn the game on and have a good time, even on days where I'm not at my best.

Pros:
-It doesn't look like something built on the Unity Engine. The quality of graphics and the fluidity of gameplay is definitely from a professional. With my current setup, I was able to lower graphics settings to the point where I achieved a stable 300+ fps. The timing windows don't necessitate a framerate that high, it's more of a testament to how much care the developer took to make sure many people can play something that looks and feels like a modern game.
-Pacing is great and the variety of upgrades, characters and moves all feel like they have their purpose. At first there were plenty of stuff that I picked up and thought "Why would I want this?" then you progress and start to see the use for that stuff and it starts to click as you play just how much variety and depth there is to the game.

I could continue to go on about what I like about this game, I look forward to even further updates on it and hope to see it get the recognition it deserves
